description_long: This object is a linear guard rail composed of four parallel horizontal bars supported by two vertical posts at each end. The bars and posts are cylindrical, likely made of metal, and are evenly spaced to prevent passage while allowing visibility through the structure. The ends of the posts extend slightly above and below the top and bottom rails, suggesting they are designed to be anchored into the ground or a base. The surface appears smooth and has a glossy black finish, indicating a protective coating, possibly powder-coated steel. There are no visible reflective markings or additional safety features on the rails. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for use along roadsides, parking lots, or pedestrian walkways to provide a physical barrier.
description: A glossy black metal guard rail with four horizontal bars and two vertical posts, designed for use as a linear barrier in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four horizontal bars and both vertical posts, highlighting the full width and structure of the guard rail.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profile of the guard rail, with only the edge of the posts and bars visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the four horizontal bars and two vertical posts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile with minimal visible detail except the post edges.
